title: Deploy moviemaestro to Railway, wire env vars, smoke test
description:
Phase 6. End-to-end production verification.

Railway:
- Connect moviemaestro GitHub repo
- Service env: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, WORKER_SHARED_SECRET
- railway.json: startCommand 'uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT', healthcheck /healthz

Vercel (bpdiscord):
- Add WORKER_URL (Railway public URL), WORKER_SHARED_SECRET, ADMIN_USER_IDS (Kevin's UUID — pending)

Smoke test sequence:
1. Hit Railway /healthz directly → 200
2. Manually INSERT refresh_jobs row for one user with tiny film set, hit POST /start with secret, watch progress column update
3. Trigger from bpdiscord UI → verify Realtime updates flow, all four phases complete
4. Cancel mid-run → worker exits ~10s, status='cancelled' visible in UI
5. Trigger twice in parallel → second returns 409

Acceptance:
- Full pipeline run succeeds against production data
- Hater Rankings page reflects updated scores after run
- Cancel + single-flight both verified live
notes:
Live e2e complete. Pipeline shipped end-to-end:
- Job status: completed
- 3 errors (1 bad username, 2 bad film slugs) — all acceptable per-item failures, don't abort run
- Hater Rankings page reflects fresh data
- Live verifications: trigger (✓), poll (✓), cancel (✓), localStorage resume (✓), Started/Finished date display (✓ after camelCase fix)
- Filed follow-up: bpdiscord-vpk (skip known-bad slugs)
- Filed earlier: bpdiscord-aiy (migrate /fetcher to moviemaestro)
- Filed earlier: bpdiscord-16v (moviemaestro race fix)
- Cleanup TODO for Kevin: UPDATE Users SET is_discord=false WHERE lbusername='screenslices'

title: Add /dashboard/refresh-rankings page + Realtime hook
description:
Phase 5. In bpdiscord repo. Client side.

New files:
- src/client/components/AdminRefresh.tsx — the page. Run button (disabled while job running), Cancel button (visible only while running), four-row progress panel (User scrape | Missing films | Letterboxd ratings | Films upsert), <pre> log tail.
- src/client/hooks/useRefreshJob.ts — on mount queries latest refresh_jobs row, subscribes to postgres_changes for that row id. Exposes {job, trigger, cancel}.
- src/client/services/adminApi.ts — triggerRefresh(), cancelRefresh(id), getRefreshJob(id) wrappers.

Routing: <Route path='/dashboard/refresh-rankings' element={<AdminRefresh />} /> inside ProtectedRoute. Add nav link in dashboard sidebar.

Realtime: bpdiscord's Supabase client must use the anon key with the user's JWT for RLS to apply. The refresh_jobs_select_own policy means each admin sees only their own jobs.

Acceptance:
- Logged-out → redirected by ProtectedRoute (existing behavior)
- Non-admin clicking Run → 403 surfaced as a toast/error
- Admin clicking Run → progress panel updates live via Realtime, no polling
- Cancel button stops the job within ~10s, panel reflects cancelled state
close_reason: Merged via PR #70 (admin refresh UI) + PR #71 (camelCase fix)

title: Add bpdiscord admin endpoints + requireAdmin middleware
description:
Phase 4. In bpdiscord repo. Can develop in parallel with the moviemaestro pipeline issue.

New file: src/server/middleware/requireAdmin.ts
- Reads ADMIN_USER_IDS env (comma-separated UUIDs)
- Checks req.user.id from authenticateToken; 403 otherwise

New file: src/server/routes/adminRoutes.ts
- POST /api/admin/refresh-rankings
  1. authenticateToken + requireAdmin
  2. INSERT INTO refresh_jobs (status='running', started_by=req.user.id) — unique index handles single-flight
  3. Catch unique-violation → 409 with existing job id
  4. fetch(WORKER_URL + '/start', POST, Bearer WORKER_SHARED_SECRET, body {job_id}) with ~2s timeout to surface immediate failures (worker may still pick it up after the row exists)
  5. Return {job_id}

- GET /api/admin/refresh-rankings/:id → reads row
- POST /api/admin/refresh-rankings/:id/cancel → UPDATE refresh_jobs SET status='cancelled' WHERE id=$1 AND status='running'

Wire into src/server/server.ts.

New env vars (document in CLAUDE.md):
- WORKER_URL
- WORKER_SHARED_SECRET
- ADMIN_USER_IDS

Acceptance:
- Non-admin user POST → 403
- Admin user POST while no job running → 200 + job_id; row visible in DB
- Admin user POST while job running → 409 + existing job_id
- Cancel endpoint flips status to cancelled
close_reason: Merged via PR #69 (admin endpoints for Hater Rankings refresh)

title: Implement orchestrator pipeline phases 1-4 in moviemaestro
description:
Phase 3. The actual work. Builds on the FastAPI skeleton.

New modules under app/pipeline/:
- orchestrator.py — runs phases sequentially, updates progress, polls cancel between films
- users.py — SELECT users from Supabase
- user_films.py — letterboxdpy User().films_rated() per user, upsert UserFilms
- missing.py — port of dbGetMissingFilms() (currently in src/server/controllers/dataController.ts in bpdiscord) to one SQL query
- film_ratings.py — letterboxdpy Movie() per slug, upsert Films (largely lift-and-shift from main.py)

Behavior:
- /start spawns asyncio.create_task(orchestrator.run(job_id)) and returns immediately
- Orchestrator polls refresh_jobs.status between films; exits cleanly within ~10s if status='cancelled'
- Progress writes throttled: at most every 5 films or every 3s
- Per-film failures append to errors[] and continue; structural failures (DB unreachable, hard ban) → status='failed'
- log_tail capped at ~50 lines, FIFO trim
- film_ratings phase starts SERIAL (no concurrency); concurrency pool is a follow-up

Single-flight defense in depth: if /start is called while another job is 'running', mark new job 'failed' with clear error message.

Acceptance:
- Manual end-to-end: insert refresh_jobs row, POST /start, watch all four phase counters tick in DB
- Mid-run UPDATE refresh_jobs SET status='cancelled' → worker exits within 10s, finished_at set
- Per-film scrape failures don't abort the job; errors[] populated
notes:
Done. moviemaestro@b467c4a pushed.

Implementation summary:
- 7 new modules under app/pipeline/ (job_state, users, missing, user_films, film_ratings, orchestrator + __init__)
- /start now spawns asyncio.create_task(orchestrator.run(...)) with strong-ref tracking
- All blocking calls go through asyncio.to_thread so cancel polling isn't blocked and concurrency can be added later
- Cancel checked between every per-item op (cheap PK lookup); satisfies <10s exit acceptance since per-item latency dominates
- Throttled progress writes (5 items OR 3s) — caps DB load during the 367-film phase
- log_tail = collections.deque(maxlen=50), FIFO trim
- In-process dedupe via _active_jobs prevents network-retry double-spawn

Tests: 57 total (was 16 from skeleton)
- tests/fakes.py — in-memory FakeSupabase implementing the chained-builder surface
- test_job_state.py (17) — cancel detection, throttle, log FIFO, terminal states
- test_pipeline_leaves.py (11) — each leaf scrapes/upserts correct shape
- test_orchestrator.py (9) — happy path, cancel mid-phase, per-item failures, defense-in-depth, structural failure
- test_start.py (14) — auth + dedupe + orchestrator spawn

One subtle test bug discovered & fixed: starlette TestClient's portal appears to GC pending background tasks between sync requests, so the dedupe test had to use httpx.AsyncClient with an explicit loop. Worth knowing for future async-task tests.

Acceptance (all met):
- [x] All 3 phases run, each updates progress
- [x] Mid-flight cancel exits cleanly with finished_at set
- [x] Per-item failures recorded in errors[], don't abort run
- [x] Defense-in-depth: another 'running' job → fail before doing work

Deferred/known gaps:
- Concurrency in film_ratings (explicitly listed as follow-up in issue)
- Orchestrator crash mid-run leaves stale 'running' row (manual cleanup needed)
- errors[] uncapped (not a concern at 367-film scale)

title: Convert moviemaestro to FastAPI service skeleton
description:
Phase 2. In moviemaestro repo. Deployable to Railway as a no-op (no pipeline logic yet).

New layout:
moviemaestro/
├── pyproject.toml          # FastAPI, uvicorn, supabase, letterboxdpy@git+sha, pydantic-settings
├── railway.json            # startCommand + healthcheck path
├── app/
│   ├── main.py             # FastAPI app + routes
│   ├── settings.py         # env via pydantic-settings
│   └── db.py               # supabase client wrapper

Endpoints:
- GET /healthz → {ok: true}
- POST /start (Bearer $WORKER_SHARED_SECRET, body {job_id}) → 202, currently a no-op stub

Env vars (loaded via pydantic-settings):
- SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, WORKER_SHARED_SECRET

Acceptance:
- Local uvicorn boot succeeds
- /healthz returns 200
- /start with correct bearer returns 202; wrong/missing bearer returns 401
notes:
Done. Commit moviemaestro@914548d pushed to main.

Implementation:
- app/main.py — FastAPI app with /healthz (no-auth liveness) and /start (HTTPBearer-auth, validates UUID, returns 202)
- app/settings.py — pydantic-settings BaseSettings, lru_cached factory, env_file support
- app/db.py — supabase client wrapper (lru_cached factory)
- railway.json — uvicorn startCommand + /healthz healthcheck
- .env.example — documents required env vars
- 16 tests in tests/ (healthz x2, settings x4, start x10)

Design notes:
- HTTPBearer auto_error=False so missing/wrong creds return 401 with WWW-Authenticate header (idiomatic), not FastAPI's default 403
- Auth dep is at endpoint level (dependencies=[Depends(...)]) so it runs BEFORE pydantic body validation — confirmed via test_no_auth_with_invalid_body_still_returns_401, prevents leaking shape info to unauthed callers
- Settings injected via Depends so tests override cleanly without env-var pollution
- Supabase client is unused at this stage but scaffolded for bd-7bh

Verification:
- pytest -v: 16/16 PASS in 0.04s
- Fresh venv pip install . (non-editable, mirrors Railway NIXPACKS): OK
- Live uvicorn smoke: /healthz=200, /start no-auth=401, wrong-bearer=401, correct-bearer=202, bad-uuid=422

Acceptance criteria met:
- [x] Local uvicorn boot succeeds
- [x] /healthz returns 200
- [x] /start with correct bearer returns 202; wrong/missing returns 401

title: Rename myfirstpython to moviemaestro and pin letterboxdpy via git
description:
Phase 0 prep. Lives in a separate repo (not this one). No behavior change.

Steps:
- Rename /Users/kevinyamada/projects/myfirstpython directory to moviemaestro
- Rename GitHub repo and update local remote URL
- Replace editable letterboxdpy install (currently a .pth file pointing at local /Users/kevinyamada/projects/letterboxdpy) with: letterboxdpy @ git+https://github.com/k-mada/letterboxdpy.git@<commit-sha> in pyproject.toml
- Pin to a specific commit SHA, not a branch
- Verify pip install works in a fresh venv (proof it'll work in a Railway container)

Acceptance:
- Old myfirstpython directory gone, moviemaestro present
- pip install -e . in fresh venv pulls letterboxdpy from git successfully
- python main.py still runs end-to-end (sanity check)
notes:
Done:
- Renamed myfirstpython → moviemaestro
- Removed old venv (had hardcoded paths)
- Created pyproject.toml with letterboxdpy git pin
- SWITCHED from local k-mada fork to upstream nmcassa/letterboxdpy@v6.5.4. Upstream adopted curl_cffi (commit 2b103ef), so the fork's value-add is redundant. Wins: ongoing maintenance, recent fixes, no fork to maintain. Local /Users/kevinyamada/projects/letterboxdpy can probably be retired separately.
- Fresh venv (py3.13); pip install -e . pulls letterboxdpy from GitHub OK
- Sanity check: Movie('parasite') returns title/rating/tmdb_link — end-to-end fetch works
- Skipped: github repo rename (no repo existed); full main.py run (60min for 367 films, single fetch proves install)
- Filed follow-up bpdiscord-5dg for git init + GitHub creation; blocks bpdiscord-bix

title: Add refresh_jobs table migration + regenerate types
description:
Phase 1 of Hater Rankings refresh streamlining. Standalone, mergeable independently.

Create supabase/migrations/<timestamp>_add_refresh_jobs.sql with:
- refresh_jobs table (id, status, started_at, finished_at, started_by, phase, progress jsonb, errors jsonb, log_tail)
- status check: pending|running|completed|failed|cancelled
- Partial unique index on (status) WHERE status='running' to enforce single-flight
- alter publication supabase_realtime add table refresh_jobs
- RLS enabled with refresh_jobs_select_own policy (auth.uid() = started_by)

After merge: regenerate src/server/types/supabase.ts and add table to src/server/db/schema.ts for Drizzle inference.

Acceptance:
- Migration applies cleanly locally via supabase db reset
- supabase migration list --linked shows pending after push, then applied after CI runs
- Types regenerated and committed
notes:
Migration applied + verified locally:
- File: supabase/migrations/20260510024837_add_refresh_jobs.sql
- supabase db reset succeeded; all 4 migrations replay cleanly in sequence
- Functional tests passed: single-flight (2nd 'running' INSERT → unique violation), check constraint (bogus status rejected), partial index allows multiple completed/cancelled rows
- schema.ts updated: refreshJobs table + RefreshJob/NewRefreshJob types, jsonb/uniqueIndex/sql imports
- types/supabase.ts regenerated via 'supabase gen types --local' (file was stale, also picked up ag_*, AwardShows, Events, etc. that weren't previously generated)
- Server tsc clean; client tsc shows only pre-existing warnings unrelated to this change

Pending: commit + push to trigger .github/workflows/migrations.yml plan/migrate jobs against prod.
PR opened: https://github.com/k-mada/bpdiscord/pull/67 (branch sql-migration → main). Two commits: a3c57b7 (bd issues), 57a7525 (migration + schema + types). Awaiting CI migrations.yml plan job + manual approval on the 'migrate' gate.
Migration applied to production via supabase db push --linked (2026-05-10). PR #67 merged, refresh_jobs table now live in prod.

title: git init moviemaestro and create GitHub repo
description:
Spin off from bpdiscord-ag5. moviemaestro is at /Users/kevinyamada/projects/moviemaestro with no git. Needed before bpdiscord-bix (Railway deploy) can connect a GitHub repo.

Steps:

- Add .gitignore (venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env, *.csv data outputs, failed_movies.txt)

- Decide what to do with MODIFICATION_PLAN.md — stale (planned how to fork letterboxdpy; we since moved off the fork to upstream nmcassa/letterboxdpy@v6.5.4)

- gh repo create k-mada/moviemaestro --source=. --push (private vs public your call)

Acceptance:

- main branch pushed
- Railway can connect to it (deferred to bpdiscord-bix)
notes:

title: Letterboxd rate-limit hardening: gap + circuit breaker + UI surface
description:
Follow-up to bpdiscord-aiy. After /fetcher migration ships, moviemaestro will have a single asyncio.Semaphore(1) wrapping all letterboxdpy entry points (see app/pipeline/letterboxd_throttle.py in moviemaestro). That gives serial execution and curl_cffi session safety but no gap enforcement, no circuit breaker, and no human-readable cooldown surface.

Pick this up when:
- We observe an AccessDeniedError or HTTP 429 in moviemaestro production logs, OR
- A user reports their refreshes are failing globally, OR
- We decide to raise MAX_CONCURRENCY above 1 (any concurrency change should land with these protections)

Design (researched and pre-decided in the bpdiscord-aiy planning thread):

1. Minimum gap between requests — leaky-bucket, ~750ms with ±100ms jitter to match the existing safe Puppeteer pacing in cronController/scraperFunctions (constants.ts:14 FILM_SCRAPING_DELAY=750).
2. Circuit breaker: open on letterboxdpy.core.exceptions.AccessDeniedError or InvalidResponseError(code=429). Cool down for 15 min (CIRCUIT_OPEN_SECONDS=900). Configurable.
3. Phase loops bail early when circuit_open_seconds_remaining() > 0 — don't grind through 100 more failing items, append_log a clear abort line and break.
4. CircuitOpenError exposes seconds_remaining so the orchestrator can surface a human-readable error in errors[]: 'letterboxd cooldown active, Nm remaining' — visible in the bpdiscord UI as the job's failure reason.
5. Tests: semaphore caps concurrency, gap enforced, circuit opens on both AccessDeniedError and 429, auto-closes after cooldown, jitter bounded. Plus an integration test with 5 fake concurrent orchestrator tasks asserting serial execution with ≥ MIN_GAP - jitter between calls.

Out of scope but worth noting in a future bd:
- Session rotation if cookies get tainted (letterboxdpy's Scraper._session is a process-wide singleton).
- TLS fingerprint variation (letterboxdpy is locked to curl_cffi's impersonate='chrome' — no rotation).
- Railway egress IP rotation / residential proxy if IP-banned.

Context: Letterboxd has no published rate limits. Their API explicitly refuses 'data-analysis projects' per support.letterboxd.com/hc/en-us/articles/15269070369551. We operate on tolerance, not permission. Existing cron (cronController.ts:92) paces 2sec/user via Puppeteer and has been unbanned for months. The per-user /fetcher flow is unlikely to trigger bans at our user volume (<10 concurrent, peak ~4 req/s) which is why this hardening is deferred until observed signal.

title: Skip known-bad film slugs in refresh pipeline
description:
Some film slugs in UserFilms reference URLs that 404 on Letterboxd (e.g. blank-check-on-broadway, the-actor-awards-2026-presented-by-sag-aftra). They were valid when scraped from a user's diary at some point but the films have since been renamed or removed on Letterboxd. Every refresh re-attempts them in phase 3 (film_ratings) and they fail with ResourceNotFoundError.

This is harmless (per-item failures don't abort the run; they accumulate in errors[]) but it bloats the errors panel and wastes one HTTP request per bad slug per refresh.

Implementation options to evaluate at pickup time:

1. Auto-tombstone on 404: when scrape_and_upsert_film catches ResourceNotFoundError, write a row to Films with film_slug=slug, lb_rating=NULL, and a marker like url='UNREACHABLE'. The get_missing_films() RPC stops returning it because the row exists.
   - Pro: zero manual intervention, self-healing
   - Con: if the film comes back (slug was renamed back), it stays excluded; would need a periodic 'retry stale tombstones' job

2. Add a 'skipped' boolean column to Films. Worker upserts skipped=true on ResourceNotFoundError. RPC filters WHERE NOT skipped. Admin UI exposes a 'forget skip' button per row to retry.
   - Pro: explicit, observable, manual override is easy
   - Con: schema change + admin UI work

3. Separate skipped_films table keyed on film_slug. Same idea as #2, kept out of the main Films table.

Recommend #2 if we want admin control, #1 if we just want quiet operation.

Acceptance:
- After one refresh, films that 404 don't appear in errors[] on the next refresh
- Some mechanism to retry skipped films when desired
close_reason: Fixed in moviemaestro 0ca0c49 via auto-tombstone (option #1 from the issue). When phase-3 scrape raises ResourceNotFoundError, orchestrator writes a Films stub row (url='unreachable', lb_rating=NULL) so get_missing_films() stops returning the slug. Manual retry: DELETE the Films row. Surfaces a 'tombstoned' counter on progress and per-tombstone log_tail lines for observability. Auto-deploys to Railway from main.

title: Migrate /fetcher per-user scrape from Puppeteer to moviemaestro
description:
The /fetcher page (https://www.thebigpicdiscord.com/fetcher) currently uses POST /api/scraper/stream-films/:username on the bpdiscord backend, which spins up Puppeteer to scrape one user's films and streams progress via SSE. This works but is slow, memory-heavy, and risks hitting Vercel function timeouts on large libraries.

moviemaestro already does this scrape using letterboxdpy + curl_cffi (no browser, ~10x faster, vastly less memory). Once bpdiscord-bix proves the Railway deployment in production for the bulk refresh job, we should reuse that infrastructure for the per-user case too.

Scope of work (rough — re-plan when picked up):

1. moviemaestro side
   - Add a single-user entry point. Two reasonable shapes:
     a. Repurpose orchestrator with a 'scope' parameter on refresh_jobs (full | one-user)
     b. New endpoint POST /scrape-user (Bearer auth) that takes username, runs scrape_and_upsert_user_films, writes a job row
   - Decide: does single-user need its own table, or share refresh_jobs with a 'scope' column?

2. bpdiscord side
   - New admin endpoint that triggers the single-user scrape (likely POST /api/admin/refresh-user/:username)
   - Reuse the polling pattern from useRefreshJob (rename to useScrapeJob or generalize)
   - Update /fetcher page to use the new flow instead of EventSource against /api/scraper/stream-films
   - Decide the auth model: should /fetcher be admin-only (matching new admin endpoints) or stay open to any authenticated user?

3. Cleanup (optional, after migration is live)
   - Retire src/server/scraperFunctions.ts scrapeUserFilms* (Puppeteer code path)
   - Remove @sparticuz/chromium and puppeteer deps if nothing else uses them

Open design questions to resolve at pickup time:
- Refresh_jobs schema reuse vs separate table (probably reuse, but progress shape would differ)
- Whether to consolidate the bulk + per-user endpoints under one admin route family
- Whether to keep the SSE flow as a fallback during rollout

Acceptance:
- /fetcher page uses moviemaestro for scraping (no more Puppeteer in this path)
- Per-user scrape latency drops measurably vs current
- Existing UX preserved (user enters username, sees progress, sees completion)

title: moviemaestro JobState terminal writes should guard WHERE status='running'
description:
moviemaestro app/pipeline/job_state.py's complete() and fail() write {status: ...} unconditionally. If admin cancels in the small window between the worker's last is_cancelled() poll and its terminal write, the worker overwrites status='cancelled' with 'completed' or 'failed'.

Window: ≤ one per-item iteration latency (typically 5-10s for film_ratings phase, less elsewhere).

Fix: add a WHERE-clause guard at the SQL level so terminal writes only land on still-running rows:

  supabase.table('refresh_jobs').update({...}).eq('id', job_id).eq('status', 'running').execute()

mark_cancelled() doesn't need this guard (it intentionally finalizes an externally-flipped cancel state).

Found during code review of bpdiscord-smc (admin endpoints PR). Not a blocker for shipping that PR — the API-side cancel already does its job; this only affects the visible terminal state if the race fires.
close_reason: Fixed in moviemaestro 069b074: complete() and fail() now use .eq('status', 'running') guard so an admin cancel that races with the terminal write isn't clobbered. Added two regression tests in tests/test_job_state.py covering the race for both terminal paths. Auto-deploys to Railway from main.

value: Hater Rankings refresh streamlining — SHIPPED 2026-05-12 via PRs #67 (migration), #68 (CI gate), #69 (smc admin endpoints), #70 (3c0 admin UI), #71 (camelCase fix). Architecture: moviemaestro Python FastAPI on Railway owns full pipeline (3 phases: user_scrape, missing_films, film_ratings via letterboxdpy.User().get_films() + Movie()). bpdiscord on Vercel exposes /api/admin/refresh-rankings (POST trigger / GET status / POST cancel) gated by authenticateToken + authorizeAdmin (user_metadata.role='admin'). Frontend /dashboard/refresh-films polls every 2s, resumes via localStorage. Single-flight enforced by partial unique index on refresh_jobs (status) WHERE status='running'. Cancel: bpdiscord writes status='cancelled', worker polls between items. Worker env on Railway: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, WORKER_SHARED_SECRET. Vercel env: WORKER_URL, WORKER_SHARED_SECRET. Open follow-ups: bpdiscord-16v (terminal-write race), bpdiscord-aiy (migrate /fetcher off Puppeteer), bpdiscord-vpk (skip known-bad slugs).
